meta.subtitle: Plain-English market read · Gap-risk check · Last session before the long Juneteenth weekend

verdict.label: YELLOW · Trade small or sit out
verdict.desc: Wednesday the new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh delivered a hawkish surprise — the Fed held rates but its dot plot flipped from cuts to possible HIKES (the 2026 median moved to 3.8% from 3.4%), and stocks fell about 1.2% while bond yields jumped. Today is the last regular session before a 3-day weekend: markets are closed Friday for Juneteenth, and the U.S.–Iran peace deal is being signed Friday in Switzerland while you can't trade. Any spread you sell today rides over the weekend with live headline risk — and today is also quarterly "triple-witching" expiration. Take one small trade at most, or simply wait until Monday.

composite.band_note: MILD · up from 44 · post-Fed shock + 3-day weekend

gap.band_note: Below the danger line · but it covers a 3-day weekend with the Iran signing

focus.jbig: Digesting a hawkish Fed · 3-day weekend ahead
focus.jtag: Warsh's dot plot flipped from cuts to possible hikes — yields jumped
focus.jnote: Today is a "wait it out" day. Wednesday's Fed was the big shock — nine of 18 officials penciled in at least one rate hike this year, the opposite of the cuts markets expected entering 2026, and Warsh said the Fed "dropped" its forward guidance. Stocks dropped about 1.2% and Treasury yields jumped (2-year +16 bps). Now you face a 3-day weekend: markets are closed Friday for Juneteenth, and the U.S.–Iran peace deal is set to be signed Friday in Switzerland with the Strait of Hormuz reopening — all while you can't trade. Today is also quarterly triple-witching, which can add choppiness. The smart move is to wait until Monday.
focus.notdown: Energy and defensives held up best relative to the tape, but there's no fresh bullish catalyst — and shorting puts into a hawkish-Fed, rising-yield backdrop is low grade. Wait.
focus.notup: Rate-sensitive growth & tech (NVDA, MSFT, META) is the cleaner read down: higher-for-longer rates pressure long-duration stocks, and Wednesday's losses were led by mega-cap tech (META −5.5%, MSFT −3.8%). But it's pre-holiday with the Iran signing looming, so size down hard.
focus.why: Why YELLOW and not RED? The raw numbers (48 and 30%) are still inside the calm zone, so this isn't a full stand-down. But two rules apply: never carry full size over a long weekend, and respect a fresh policy shock. The Fed just flipped from cuts to possible hikes and the Iran deal signs Friday while the market is closed. Take one small, well-supported trade if it checks out at 9:35 AM, or just wait. A cleaner read returns Monday 6/22 once the weekend headlines are priced in.

how_built.name: Volatility — VIX ~18.4, still moderate but up ~12% on the Fed
Big events (next 1–3 days) — 3-day weekend + Iran signing Friday while closed + triple-witching today
World risk — Iran deal a 60-day framework, not a final settlement; Hormuz reopening still uncertain
Volatility trend — ticking up after the hawkish dot plot
Commodities — oil at 3-month lows (~$76), calming for inflation

snapshot.delta: all 11 sectors fell · mega-cap tech led down
3-month low · Iran deal signed, Hormuz reopens
↑ jumped on hawkish dot plot (2Y +16bp to 4.21%)

setups.read: Higher-for-longer rates pressure long-duration tech, and these led Wed's drop (META −5.5%, MSFT −3.8%). But shorting into a fast move right before a 3-day weekend with the Iran signing is risky. Half size at most.

Oil is at 3-month lows and the Iran deal is being signed Friday, which caps the group — but the signing happens over the closed weekend, so headlines could whipsaw oil Monday. Stand down into the holiday.
Small caps lag on rising yields, but that's a macro drift, not a single named catalyst. Not a clean Sniper setup. Wait.
Relative safety in a risk-off tape, but rising yields hurt rate-proxy utilities and there's no fresh catalyst or built support. Low grade.
Brand-new IPO (public June 12): no options history, no support levels, parabolic post-IPO move. Can't use it.

scenarios.label: Most likely — Quiet, choppy digestion, then the long weekend
scenarios.sub: Market absorbs the hawkish Fed; triple-witching adds noise; everyone flat into Friday's close

scenarios.label: Bad case — Yields keep rising, tech keeps sliding
scenarios.sub: Hike fears stick → more pressure on long-duration growth and small caps

scenarios.label: Good case — Oversold bounce, dip-buyers step in
scenarios.sub: Stocks stabilize as oil at 3-month lows eases the inflation story → relief rally

scenarios.label: Surprise — Iran signing stumbles over the weekend
scenarios.sub: Geneva ceremony slips or Hormuz reopening stalls → oil & vol gap Monday

risks: 3-day weekend gap risk · markets CLOSED Fri 6/19 (Juneteenth) — any spread you hold rides through Friday and the weekend with no ability to trade; today is also quarterly triple-witching.

Iran deal signs Friday in Switzerland while the market is closed — the interim deal reopens the Strait of Hormuz and resumes Iranian oil exports, but it's a 60-day framework, not a final settlement; oil could gap Monday.

Hawkish dot plot · cuts flipped to possible hikes — nine of 18 officials penciled in at least one 2026 hike and the median rate forecast rose to 3.8%; growth and tech can keep dropping if yields rise further.
10-year yield elevated (~4.46–4.50%) — rising rates keep pressuring long-duration tech and small caps.
Warsh dropped forward guidance — the Fed said it "can't give guidance on what's next," raising policy uncertainty and the odds of bigger surprises ahead.

rows.today: Broad risk-off: SPX −1.2%, all 11 sectors down

rows.delta: hold fire over the long weekend
